text:
Glitch and Noise: The Aesthetic Heartbeat of Hyperpop’s Sound

Hyperpop is CHAOS on steroids. Glitch and noise are its heartbeat, driving every wild, brain-melting track. It’s giving full-on digital meltdown, and the girls that get it, GET IT 💅💥. But, listen, hyperpop didn’t just show up out of nowhere—nah, it’s rooted in the glitchy madness of the ‘90s and 2000s, when artists started taking broken sounds and making them hit different 🔊. This was when people stopped caring about being “clean” and decided that noise, distortion, and straight-up digital chaos were the new wave. 

Back in the ‘90s, artists like Aphex Twin were taking broken files, distorted sounds, and ERRORS and flipping them into something iconic 💿. Glitch was a whole *movement*, babe. Hyperpop just looked at that and said, “Let’s go harder.” Then came the 2000s, and the digital world fully exploded, with glitch getting WILDER and messier. Hyperpop? It’s the final boss of all that. Glitch, noise, and digital overload became a whole lifestyle. 💣⚡

Some tracks that laid the groundwork for this glitchy madness? First up, **Britney Spears’ “Toxic”**. The production on that track was UNREAL, with that screechy string riff slicing through everything like a glitch in the matrix ⚔️. And that breakdown? Yeah, it had the blueprint for the future. Stream the inspo here: [Toxic - Britney Spears](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LOZuxwVk7TU) 💅🔥

Then there’s **Aphex Twin’s “Windowlicker”**. This track is basically the OG of glitch. It’s got distorted beats, flipped vocals, and an energy that feels like your computer’s about to crash—but in the best way possible. Straight-up chaos 👾. This is the digital madness hyperpop lives for: [Windowlicker - Aphex Twin](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7MBaEEODzU0) 💿💥

Next, **Daft Punk’s “Harder, Better, Faster, Stronger”** was basically glitch-pop before hyperpop was even a THING. The chopped, autotuned vocals? That robotic energy? It’s the future, babe 🤖. Catch those vibes here: [Harder, Better, Faster, Stronger - Daft Punk](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gAjR4_CbPpQ) 🛸✨

AND THEN **Charli XCX’s “Vroom Vroom”**… let’s be real, this is where modern hyperpop was basically born 🚗💨. SOPHIE’s metallic, hyper-digital production on this track set the stage for what was to come. It’s giving fast, glitchy, digital RUSH. Stream and prepare to ascend: [Vroom Vroom - Charli XCX](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jQoXwBzPUSw) 💣⚡

But we can’t talk about this scene without shouting out **Brokencyde’s “Freaxxx”**. THEY were out here blending crunkcore with glitchy, auto-tuned SCREAMING before we even had a word for it 🔥. They gave us chaotic energy that was a total mood, and you can feel their influence all over hyperpop. This is early messiness that paved the way: [Freaxxx - Brokencyde](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=N8F5YSA1Oz0) 💀🔊

By the time the 2000s rolled in, the underground scene was getting LOUD and glitchy af. People were vibing with the whole digital overload life, and the culture just kept glitching out. Then you had people like SOPHIE, 100 gecs, and A.G. Cook who took all those glitch/noise vibes and maxed them out in a way that slaps HARD 🔊💥. Hyperpop was born out of that chaos—it’s the sound of the internet having a breakdown, but make it FASHION 💅✨.

Glitch and noise aren’t just random effects. They are the SOUL of hyperpop. It’s a full-on glitchcore takeover, turning the madness of living in a hyperconnected, overstimulated world into something that SLAYS. This is the sound of the future, babe, and we’re all just living in its pixelated, distorted world 🌐🔥💿.
